reference_output.believes: Andre believes the M3 long-horizon pattern (12h paper reproduction, 24h CUDA optimization, doesn't bail at plateaus) transfers to EA work — long-tail tasks with sparse signal are the EA's natural habitat, and most models give up at submission 30, M3's best came at submission 145. The position is in [[Long-Horizon Patterns]] and [[M3 Capabilities]].
reference_output.contradicts: The position is challenged by the M3 Eval Lab status (0 runs) — the long-horizon capability is observed on paper reproduction and CUDA optimization, not on EA work. EA long-tail is a different distribution (sparse signal, subjective success criteria, multi-day timescales). [[Mavis EA Workflow]] also implicitly assumes long-horizon, but has no measurement of M3's actual behavior on EA long-tail.

The M3 Eval Lab is the design for this. Also missing: any comparison to operator-led long-tail (Andre's own 12h deep-work sessions) — what does Mavis do that Andre cannot, and vice versa.
reference_output.unasked_question: If M3 can run 12h autonomously on a controlled task (paper reproduction), what is the analogous controlled task for EA work that would let us measure long-horizon behavior in a single session?

topic: The vault as a knowledge graph and the linking discipline
vault_state_summary: Andre has 5 notes: Linking Principle, CHIEF as Codebase Pattern, Mavis-Apex-Map, Vault Conventions, Linking Principle Reflection. 1 connection (Composability wins on MCPs).
question: Run deep research. Output: believes, contradicts, missing, unasked question.

reference_output.believes: Andre believes the vault is a knowledge graph where the value is in the edges, not the nodes — link on reference, link on processing, link on review, no orphan notes, every permanent note connects to ≥1 other note. The position is in [[Linking Principle]] and [[Vault Conventions]]: 'the network is the value.'
reference_output.contradicts: The position is challenged by [[Linking Principle Reflection]] — the audit revealed the Mavis-Apex-Map canvas is missing backlinks to the 4 doc files, and the INDEX dataview block should pull from MOCs not the root. The principle is sound; the discipline of execution has gaps. [[Composability wins on MCPs]] also implicitly contradicts: a knowledge graph with a tool surface problem (vault-brain doesn't exist) is a degraded graph.
reference_output.missing: Andre has 0 notes on the linking quality itself — are all the existing links load-bearing, or are some decorative? The Linking Principle is 'link on reference' but doesn't distinguish a 1-line reference link from a 5-sentence bridging link. Also missing: any tooling for link audit (orphan detection, broken links, MOC freshness).
reference_output.unasked_question: If the value is in the edges, what is the right metric for edge quality — and is it link count, link density, link freshness, or something else?

topic: Whole-vault in-context as the design primitive
vault_state_summary: Andre has 4 notes: Paged Memory Pattern, Context Engineering 1M, Mavis-Apex-Architecture, M3 Capabilities. 1 question (Does M3 Hold 1M Context).
question: Run deep research. Output: believes, contradicts, missing, unasked question.

reference_output.believes: Andre believes whole-vault-in-context is the right design primitive for M3 — load the whole vault, partition by relevance, never chunk-and-embed by default. The M3 1M context makes this possible; the 200K-token budget for retrieval respects the context window. The position is in [[Mavis-Apex-Architecture]] and [[Paged Memory Pattern]]: 'external state is for staging, not for truth.'
reference_output.contradicts: The position is challenged by [[Does M3 Hold 1M Context]] — if the 1M context degrades in the middle, whole-vault is a degraded primitive. [[MSA Signal Decay]] asks whether the architecture introduces silent signal decay that makes 'whole-vault' a misnomer. The position also assumes the operator can curate what 200K to load — the Long Context Curator is the design, but the build is unstarted.
reference_output.missing: Andre has 0 notes on what 'whole-vault' actually means at 2K notes — the vault is 2K notes, ~5M tokens, well over 1M. The primitive is 'whole-vault' but the budget is 200K. The actual primitive is 'top-20-full-notes' or 'top-200-chunks-aggregated', not 'whole-vault'. The naming is aspirational. Also missing: any benchmark comparing top-20-full-notes vs top-200-chunks on EA synthesis tasks.
reference_output.unasked_question: If 'whole-vault' is the aspiration but 'top-20-full-notes' is the reality, is the right primitive the budget-controlled retrieval or the curator that decides which 20?

topic: Direct-Intake MCP and the passive capture problem
vault_state_summary: Andre has 2 notes: Direct Intake MCP Design, M3 Edge. 1 question (How to Avoid Inbox Fill). 1 pattern (Show Don't Describe).
question: Run deep research. Output: believes, contradicts, missing, unasked question.

reference_output.believes: Andre believes the Direct-Intake MCP is the right solution to the passive capture problem — screenshots, voice memos, bookmarked links currently miss the passive intelligence because the intake path is 'Andre tells Mavis, Mavis writes to vault.' The MCP watches a folder, processes whatever lands (M3-native vision, audio, link), and writes a processed note to 00 Inbox/ with pre-suggested tags and links. The position is in [[Direct Intake MCP Design]]: 'show, don't describe applied to my own intake.'
reference_output.contradicts: The position is challenged by [[How to Avoid Inbox Fill]] itself — every passive capture system dies of inbox fill, and the design has 6 defenses (hard confidence floor, dedup, batch processing, rate cap, tag garbage collection, dashboard) but none are battle-tested. [[M3 Edge]] makes the native multimodal primitive possible but doesn't guarantee the operator layer (Mavis) can handle the intake rate.
reference_output.missing: Andre has 0 notes on the operator cost of passive capture — what does Mavis do with 50 dropped files per day? The design is 0.85 confidence floor, but the operator is also reading daily briefs, processing inbox, surfacing connections. The passive capture adds to the operator's load. Also missing: any note on the privacy implication (M3 sees screenshots of Slack threads, voice memos that may contain PII).
reference_output.unasked_question: If the operator is the bottleneck on intake, what is the right rate cap — and is it '20 per hour' (technical) or '20 per session' (operator-aware)?

The principle is sound; the practice is reactive, not proactive. The M3 Eval Lab is 0 runs; the disease checks are unstarted.

The mapping is unstarted. Also missing: any tooling for the recurring health check.

topic: Native multimodal capture and the show-don't-describe principle

question: Run deep research. Output: believes, contradicts, missing, unasked question.

reference_output.believes: Andre believes the native multimodal primitive (M3 reads images directly, watches video, listens to audio) is the right substrate for 'show, don't describe' intake — instead of OCR pipelines + Whisper + ffmpeg, drop a screenshot and M3 sees the Slack thread, drop a voice memo and M3 hears the tone. The position is in [[M3 Edge]] and [[Multimodal GUI Loop]].
reference_output.contradicts: The position is challenged by the operational reality — the Direct-Intake MCP design is at the 0.85 confidence floor and 20-per-hour rate cap. The native capability is unlimited; the operator layer is constrained. [[Native > Wrapper]] is the principle, but the principle doesn't address operator rate limits. Also, the privacy implications of M3 seeing all screenshots are unexamined.
reference_output.missing: Andre has 0 notes on the operator workflow when passive capture is live — what does a Mavis session look like when 20 files have dropped overnight? The design is for the intake path, not the consumption path. Also missing: any note on the failure mode of passive capture (a dropped screenshot that's actually a sensitive Slack thread, a voice memo that contains PII).
reference_output.unasked_question: If the native multimodal primitive is unlimited but the operator rate is capped, is the rate cap the right design — or should the rate cap be on the operator's consumption, not the system's intake?
